title: InCoder-32B: Code Foundation Model for Industrial Scenarios
task: Code generation for industrial domains
model: (transformer) InCoder-32B
inputs: natural language descriptions of programming tasks in specialized domains
outputs: code solutions for chip design, GPU kernels, embedded systems, compiler optimization, and 3D modeling
key_results: competitive performance on 14 general code benchmarks and strong baselines on 9 industrial benchmarks; extends context from 8K to 128K tokens
comments: Notable for combining general pretraining with domain-specific annealing and execution-grounded verification, addressing a real gap in industrial code generation.

title: MiroThinker-1.7 & H1: Towards Heavy-Duty Research Agents via Verification
task: Complex long-horizon reasoning for research tasks
model: (transformer) MiroThinker-1.7 & MiroThinker-H1
inputs: user queries and tool APIs for open-web research, scientific reasoning, and financial analysis
outputs: structured reasoning plans with verified intermediate steps and final answers
key_results: state-of-the-art performance on deep research tasks with local and global-level verification; open-source 1.7-mini variant available
comments: Integration of verification into reasoning is commendable, though the specific performance metrics relative to prior work are not quantified in the abstract.

title: Demystifing Video Reasoning
task: Understanding reasoning mechanisms in video generation models
model: (diffusion transformer) video diffusion models
inputs: prompts or conditioning signals for video generation tasks requiring reasoning
outputs: generated video frames with reasoning across diffusion denoising steps
key_results: identified Chain-of-Steps (CoS) as primary reasoning mechanism instead of Chain-of-Frames; ensemble latent trajectories improve performance
comments: Provides valuable mechanistic insights into how diffusion models reason, but the practical improvements from proposed training-free strategies are not quantified.

title: WorldCam: Interactive Autoregressive 3D Gaming Worlds with Camera Pose as a Unifying Geometric Representation
task: Interactive 3D gaming world generation with precise action control
model: (diffusion transformer) video diffusion transformer
inputs: user actions represented as 6-DoF camera poses in Lie algebra; historical observations indexed by global camera pose
outputs: video frames of interactive gaming environment with 3D spatial consistency over long horizons
key_results: substantially outperforms state-of-the-art in action controllability and 3D spatial consistency; introduces 3,000-minute authentic gameplay dataset with camera trajectories
comments: Clever use of camera pose as unifying geometric representation is elegant, though quantitative comparisons on standard metrics are absent from abstract.

title: Thinking in Uncertainty: Mitigating Hallucinations in MLRMs with Latent Entropy-Aware Decoding
task: Mitigating hallucinations in visual question answering by MLRMs
model: (transformer) multimodal large reasoning models
inputs: images and questions; token probability distributions during high-entropy reasoning stages
outputs: answers with reduced hallucinations via entropy-aware decoding
key_results: LEAD strategy effectively mitigates hallucinations across multiple MLLMs and benchmarks; uses probability-weighted embeddings under high entropy
comments: Interesting approach leveraging entropy patterns in transition words, but specific quantitative improvements are not provided in abstract.

title: TRUST-SQL: Tool-Integrated Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning for Text-to-SQL over Unknown Schemas
task: Text-to-SQL parsing with unknown database schemas
model: (RL with GRPO) agent-based system with LLM backbone
inputs: natural language queries; access to unknown schemas via tool calls
outputs: executable SQL queries with metadata verification
key_results: 30.6% absolute improvement for 4B variant, 16.6% for 8B; matches schema-prefilled baselines without pre-loaded metadata
comments: Dual-Track GRPO with token-level masked advantages is novel; 9.9% relative improvement over standard GRPO demonstrates effective credit assignment.

title: Online Experiential Learning for Language Models
task: Continuous online improvement of language models from deployment experience
model: (transformer) language models at multiple scales
inputs: interaction trajectories from real-world deployment; user-side experience
outputs: improved model parameters via on-policy context distillation
key_results: consistent improvements over iterations; enhanced task accuracy and token efficiency while preserving OOD performance on text-based games
comments: Clean separation of knowledge extraction and parameter consolidation is appealing, but experiments are limited to game environments rather than broader deployment scenarios.

title: FinToolBench: Evaluating LLM Agents for Real-World Financial Tool Use
task: Evaluating LLM agents for real-world financial tool use
model: (LLM agents) various foundation models with financial tool APIs
inputs: financial queries requiring tool execution; 760 executable financial tools
outputs: evaluation scores on timeliness, intent type, and regulatory alignment; FATR baseline results
key_results: FinToolBench comprises 760 executable tools with 295 tool-required queries; introduces finance-critical evaluation dimensions beyond binary success
comments: Valuable contribution addressing real-world finance domain, though this is primarily a benchmark paper without strong baseline results reported.

title: AgentProcessBench: Diagnosing Step-Level Process Quality in Tool-Using Agents
task: Evaluating step-level effectiveness in tool-using agent trajectories
model: (LLM agents) various policy models
inputs: tool-augmented trajectories with 8,509 human-labeled step annotations
outputs: per-step correctness labels and agent quality metrics
key_results: identified key challenges: weaker models show inflated step correctness due to early termination; process signals complement outcome supervision for test-time scaling
comments: First benchmark dedicated to step-level evaluation in realistic tool use; ternary labeling scheme and error propagation rules improve annotation reliability.

title: SocialOmni: Benchmarking Audio-Visual Social Interactivity in Omni Models
task: Evaluating audio-visual social interactivity in omni-modal models
model: (multimodal) omni-modal large language models
inputs: audio-visual dialogue with speaker cues and interruption opportunities
outputs: speaker identification, interruption timing, and natural interruption generation
key_results: decoupling between perceptual accuracy and interaction generation quality across 12 OLMs; significant variance in social-interaction capabilities
comments: Operationalizes underexplored dimension of conversational AI; reveals important gap between understanding and appropriate social response generation.

title: SegviGen: Repurposing 3D Generative Model for Part Segmentation
task: 3D part segmentation via repurposing 3D generative models
model: (3D generative model) pretrained native 3D generator with segmentation adapter
inputs: 3D geometry; optional interactive or 2D guidance
outputs: part segmentation masks via distinctive part colorization
key_results: 40% improvement on interactive part segmentation, 15% on full segmentation; uses only 0.32% of labeled training data versus prior work
comments: Clever repurposing of pretrained priors for a discriminative task; significant data efficiency gain validates transferability of 3D generative models.

title: M^3: Dense Matching Meets Multi-View Foundation Models for Monocular Gaussian Splatting SLAM
task: Monocular video SLAM with dense correspondence matching
model: (vision foundation model + Gaussian splatting) M³ system combining multi-view foundation model matching with SLAM
inputs: uncalibrated monocular video stream
outputs: camera poses and 3D scene reconstruction via Gaussian splatting
key_results: 64.3% ATE RMSE reduction vs VGGT-SLAM 2.0; 2.11 dB PSNR improvement over ARTDECO on ScanNet++
comments: Augments multi-view foundation models with dedicated matching head for fine-grained correspondences; addresses critical bottleneck in geometric optimization.

title: Reliable Reasoning in SVG-LLMs via Multi-Task Multi-Reward Reinforcement Learning
task: SVG generation with structured reasoning and code refinement
model: (vision-language model with chain-of-thought) CTRL-S using GRPO and multi-reward optimization
inputs: text descriptions or images
outputs: SVG code with group-level structure and visual SVG rendering
key_results: higher task success rates, superior code quality, and exceptional visual fidelity; multi-reward framework incorporates DINO, image-text similarity, format, and efficiency rewards
comments: Introduction of chain-of-thought reasoning for SVG generation is novel; multi-reward optimization with GRPO demonstrates effective handling of competing objectives.

title: One-Eval: An Agentic System for Automated and Traceable LLM Evaluation
task: Automating LLM evaluation from natural-language requests
model: (agentic system) LLM-based evaluation orchestrator
inputs: natural-language evaluation requests; optional human edits and rollback
outputs: executable evaluation workflows with task-aware metrics and decision-oriented reporting
key_results: end-to-end evaluation execution from diverse NL requests with minimal user effort; human-in-the-loop checkpoints preserve auditability
comments: Addresses real pain point of heterogeneous evaluation codebases; focus on reproducibility and human oversight is commendable for industrial settings.

title: Semi-Autonomous Formalization of the Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau Equilibrium
task: Formal verification of plasma physics equilibrium in Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau system
model: (AI reasoning + symbolic proof) Gemini DeepThink + Claude + Aristotle prover + Lean 4 kernel
inputs: mathematical conjectures about plasma equilibrium
outputs: fully formal Lean 4 proofs verified by kernel
key_results: complete formalization of VML equilibrium characterization; 229 human prompts, 213 commits, $200 cost, 10 days with one mathematician
comments: Remarkable demonstration of AI-assisted formal verification; detailed documentation of failure modes (hypothesis creep, definition bugs, agent avoidance) provides valuable lessons for future work.

title: SK-Adapter: Skeleton-Based Structural Control for Native 3D Generation
task: Skeletal control for native 3D object generation
model: (3D diffusion model) frozen generative backbone with lightweight SK-Adapter
inputs: 3D skeleton (joint coordinates and topology) as structural control signal
outputs: high-quality 3D meshes with precise skeletal articulation and preserved texture
key_results: robust structural control while preserving geometry and texture quality; enables local 3D editing via skeletal guidance; Objaverse-TMS dataset released with 24k samples
comments: Treating 3D skeleton as first-class control signal is intuitive; lightweight adapter design preserves generative priors while enabling precise manipulation.

title: From Passive Observer to Active Critic: Reinforcement Learning Elicits Process Reasoning for Robotic Manipulation
task: Process monitoring and failure detection for long-horizon robotic manipulation
model: (video MLLM with RL) PRIMO R1 (7B), transforms passive observer to active critic
inputs: video sequences anchored between initial and current state; optional task goal
outputs: progress estimation and failure detection via chain-of-thought reasoning
key_results: 50% reduction in MAE vs specialized baselines; 67.0% accuracy on RoboFail, surpassing OpenAI o1 by 6.0%; strong zero-shot generalization
comments: Clever use of structured temporal input anchoring; outcome-based RL for eliciting process reasoning is an effective way to shift from passive recognition to active evaluation.

title: Efficient Reasoning on the Edge
task: Efficient on-device reasoning for small language models via adapter tuning and budget forcing
model: (adapter-based) LoRA adapters on small LLMs (Qwen2.5-7B) with dynamic switching
inputs: prompts requiring reasoning; budget constraints for token generation and memory
outputs: concise reasoning traces and final answers optimized for edge deployment
key_results: practical reasoning on mobile with strict resource constraints; dynamic adapter switching and KV-cache sharing reduce overhead; 20x inference speedup potential
comments: Practical contribution addressing real constraint of on-device deployment; budget forcing via RL to compress reasoning while maintaining accuracy is pragmatic.

title: Recursive Language Models Meet Uncertainty: The Surprising Effectiveness of Self-Reflective Program Search for Long Context
task: Long-context information extraction via self-reflective program search
model: (recursive decomposition) SRLM augmenting Recursive Language Models with uncertainty-aware self-reflection
inputs: long-context documents; prompts requiring reasoning over extended context
outputs: answers derived from recursively decomposed context interactions
key_results: up to 22% improvement over RLM under same time budget; self-reflection (consistency, reasoning length, confidence) outperforms recursion alone across diverse benchmarks
comments: Important finding that self-reflection is more critical than recursion itself; demonstrates that heuristic signals can guide effective long-context handling.

title: MolmoB0T: Large-Scale Simulation Enables Zero-Shot Manipulation
task: Zero-shot sim-to-real transfer for robot manipulation
model: (vision-language policy) MolmoBot based on Molmo2 with flow-matching action head; plus Pi0-replica and SPOC variants
inputs: visual observations from robot cameras; task descriptions
outputs: continuous action commands for articulated manipulation and mobile manipulation
key_results: 79.2% success on tabletop pick-and-place in real world; outperforms π₀.₅ at 39.2%; zero-shot transfer to unseen objects without real-world fine-tuning; 1.8M synthetic trajectories
comments: Strong result demonstrating that procedural sim-to-real gap can be bridged with sufficient scale and diversity; release of 1.8M trajectories and open-source pipeline valuable for community.

title: Anticipatory Planning for Multimodal AI Agents
task: Multi-step task planning for multimodal agents
model: (reinforcement learning) TraceR1
inputs: Computer-use and tool-use task instructions
outputs: Action sequences with anticipated future states
key_results: Substantial improvements in planning stability and execution robustness across seven benchmarks; improved generalization over reactive baselines
comments: Two-stage RL approach (trajectory-level + grounded fine-tuning) is well-motivated, though absolute performance metrics are not quantified in the abstract.

title: Learning Human-Object Interaction for 3D Human Pose Estimation from LiDAR Point Clouds
task: 3D human pose estimation from LiDAR under human-object interactions
model: (point cloud network) HOIL framework with contrastive learning and attention pooling
inputs: LiDAR point clouds of humans interacting with objects
outputs: 3D keypoint coordinates for human pose; temporal refinement via contact cues
key_results: HOIL effectively resolves spatial ambiguity and class imbalance in interaction regions; robust pose estimation in cluttered backgrounds; contact-aware temporal refinement improves per-frame predictions
comments: Well-motivated approach addressing two concrete challenges (spatial ambiguity, class imbalance) in autonomous driving context; temporal refinement is practical addition.

title: Polyglot-Lion: Efficient Multilingual ASR for Singapore via Balanced Fine-Tuning of Qwen3-ASR
task: Multilingual automatic speech recognition for Singapore languages
model: (fine-tuned ASR) Qwen3-ASR-0.6B and 1.7B with balanced language sampling
inputs: speech audio in English, Mandarin, Tamil, Malay
outputs: transcribed text with implicit language identification from audio
key_results: Polyglot-Lion-1.7B achieves 14.85 average error rate competitive with 6x larger MERaLiON-2-10B (14.32); 20x faster inference; $81 training cost vs $18,862
comments: Impressive efficiency gains through balanced fine-tuning strategy; elimination of language tags forces implicit learning, improving cross-lingual generalization.

title: ViT-AdaLA: Adapting Vision Transformers with Linear Attention
task: Efficient adaptation of Vision Transformers with linear attention
model: (Vision Transformer) ViT-AdaLA
inputs: Vision foundation models and downstream vision tasks
outputs: Linearized ViT parameters for classification and segmentation
key_results: Effective knowledge transfer from softmax ViTs to linear attention variants; demonstrated on classification and segmentation tasks
comments: Three-stage alignment strategy (attention, feature, fine-tuning) addresses layer-wise error accumulation, but specific performance gains versus baselines are not reported in abstract.

title: FlashSampling: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Sampling
task: Fast and memory-efficient categorical sampling for LLM decoding
model: (GPU kernel) fused sampling primitive integrating into LM-head matmul
inputs: logits tile-by-tile on-chip; Gumbel noise injection
outputs: exact categorical samples without materializing full logits tensor in HBM
key_results: up to 19% reduction in time per output token in vLLM; speeds up kernel-level decode across H100, H200, B200, B300 GPUs
comments: Elegant kernel-level optimization exploiting argmax properties; demonstrates that exact sampling can be efficiently fused into matmul without approximation.

title: CCTU: A Benchmark for Tool Use under Complex Constraints
task: Evaluating LLM tool use under complex constraints
model: (LLM agents) nine state-of-the-art models in thinking and non-thinking modes
inputs: tool-use scenarios with average 7 constraint types; prompts exceeding 4,500 tokens; 12 constraint categories across 4 dimensions
outputs: execution traces with step-level constraint violation validation
key_results: no model achieves >20% completion rate under strict constraint adherence; models violate constraints in >50% of cases; limited self-refinement capability even with feedback
comments: Sobering benchmark revealing critical gap in constraint compliance; dimensional taxonomy (resource, behavior, toolset, response) provides useful structure for future improvements.

title: OneWorld: Taming Scene Generation with 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder
task: 3D scene generation with cross-view consistency via unified 3D representation
model: (3D diffusion) OneWorld using 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder (3D-URAE) with pretrained 3D foundation models
inputs: scene descriptions or conditions; token-level cross-view correspondence loss
outputs: high-quality 3D scenes with superior cross-view appearance and geometric consistency
key_results: superior cross-view consistency vs 2D-based methods; token-level CVC consistency loss enforces structural alignment; Manifold-Drift Forcing mitigates train-inference bias
comments: Operating diffusion directly in unified 3D space addresses fundamental limitation of 2D latent approaches; augmentation of geometry-centric models with appearance and semantics is principled.

title: Measuring Primitive Accumulation: An Information-Theoretic Approach to Capitalist Enclosure in PIK2, Indonesia
task: Quantifying capitalist land enclosure via information-geometric and statistical-mechanical analysis
model: (information geometry + Markov chains) Fisher-Rao geodesics on Marxian probability simplex; percolation and fractal analysis
inputs: Sentinel-2 LULC satellite data (2017-2024) at 10m resolution; land-use fractions (Commons, Agrarian, Capital)
outputs: quantitative characterization of spatial transformation velocity, topology, irreversibility; percolation thresholds and fractal dimensions
key_results: transformation pulse of 0.405 rad/yr (2019-2020); absorption times of 46.0 yrs (cropland) and 38.1 yrs (tree cover); built-area self-retention 96.4%; urban boundary fractal dimension 1.316→1.397
comments: Novel application of mathematical physics tools to political economy; information-geometric approach to quantifying enclosure is creative but requires deep domain expertise to validate causal claims.

title: Test-Time Strategies for More Efficient and Accurate Agentic RAG
task: Improving efficiency and accuracy of iterative RAG systems
model: (LLM agent) Search-R1 with modifications
inputs: Complex multi-hop questions
outputs: Retrieved documents and generated answers with reasoning
key_results: 5.6% increase in EM score and 10.5% reduction in retrieval turns compared to Search-R1 baseline on HotpotQA and Natural Questions
comments: Test-time modifications are practical and yield concrete improvements, but the approach is incremental optimization rather than fundamental architectural innovation.

title: Analytical investigation of soliton dynamics in the Fokas–Lenells equation with generalized self-phase modulation and nonlinear dispersion
task: Analytical solution of soliton dynamics in nonlinear optical media
model: (analytical) Fokas–Lenells equation with generalized self-phase modulation and nonlinear dispersion
inputs: Quadratic-cubic self-phase modulation and chromatic dispersion parameters
outputs: Family of soliton solutions (dark, periodic, singular, dark-singular, singular-periodic)
key_results: Extended simple equation method provides streamlined solutions; modified extended tanh method generates richer spectrum; optimized approach enables soliton regulation for fiber-optic and ultrafast laser applications
comments: Three independent analytical methods confirm robustness of solution families, though practical experimental validation against these predictions is not discussed.
hypotheses: Generalized quadratic-cubic SPM and nonlinear dispersion interaction produces diverse soliton structures
Modified extended tanh method yields more compact solutions than alternative approaches
Analytical solutions enable control of pulse compression and spectrum broadening in photonic devices
results: Rich family of soliton solutions confirmed across three methods
Extended simple equation method provides straightforward implementation
Modified extended tanh generates non-redundant compact forms

title: Oxygen-controlled IGZO channel deposition for enhanced memory window in ferroelectric FETs
task: Enhancement of ferroelectric FET memory window via oxygen stoichiometry control
model: (experiment) IGZO/HfZrO2 ferroelectric field-effect transistor with oxygen-controlled deposition
inputs: Oxygen partial pressure (PO2) during IGZO channel deposition (0–20%)
outputs: Memory window width, oxygen vacancy concentration, interfacial defect density, switching speed
key_results: Optimized 5% PO2 achieves 1.85 V maximum memory window; minimizes interfacial defect states while maintaining carrier density; accelerates polarization switching compared to non-optimal conditions
comments: Systematic PO2 tuning effectively decouples oxygen stoichiometry from performance, though scaling to commercial endurance specs (write cycles) is not quantified.
hypotheses: Oxygen partial pressure modulation controls oxygen vacancy concentration in IGZO channel
Optimized interfacial defect states at IGZO/HfZrO2 maximize memory window
Precise oxygen stoichiometry enables faster nucleation-limited polarization switching
results: 5% PO2 yields maximum 1.85 V memory window
XPS confirms PO2-dependent oxygen vacancy and interfacial defect modulation
Nucleation-limited switching analysis shows accelerated dynamics at optimized condition

title: Proton channels govern vesicular carbonate chemistry in mineralizing cells of a marine calcifier
task: Elucidate proton channel control of carbonate chemistry in calcifying vesicles
model: (experiment) sea urchin embryo live-cell imaging with electrophysiology
inputs: Sea urchin calcifying cells, membrane potential, Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ ions, pH gradients
outputs: Intra-vesicular pH and Ca²⁺ concentrations, proton conductance, vesicle exocytosis dynamics
key_results: Otop2l channel activation by alkaline pH, Ca²⁺, and Mg²⁺ creates highly alkaline (>pH 8) conditions in ACC-forming vesicles; high proton permeability drives pH control at mineralization front
comments: The identification of Otop2l as a master pH regulator provides mechanistic insight into biomineralization robustness and suggests vulnerability to ocean acidification through impaired channel function.
hypotheses: Proton channels regulate intra-vesicular alkalinity during ACC formation
Otop2l is the primary proton conductor in calcifying cells
Alkaline pH and divalent cation concentration are coupled through Otop2l activation
Vesicular pH control is essential for preventing unwanted mineralization
results: Demonstrated high proton permeability in calcifying cells and ACC vesicles
Confirmed Otop2l as master regulator of membrane proton conductance
Showed Otop2l activation by alkaline conditions, Ca²⁺, and Mg²⁺
Observed correlation between membrane potential-driven proton conductance and carbonate chemistry control

title: 5-HMF inhibits glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis through the VEGFR2/PI3K/AKT pathway
task: Prevent glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis via osteoblast protection
model: (experiment) MC3T3-E1 pre-osteoblasts, BMSCs, dexamethasone-induced mouse model
inputs: 5-HMF compound; dexamethasone treatment
outputs: Osteogenic differentiation markers, bone density, bone architecture, VEGFR2/PI3K/AKT pathway activation
key_results: 5-HMF enhanced VEGFR2 phosphorylation, restored angiogenesis, activated PI3K/AKT signaling; MK2206 (AKT inhibitor) abrogated protective effects
comments: Network pharmacology prediction complements functional validation, though the in vivo quantitative bone density improvements are not explicitly stated numerically.
hypotheses: 5-HMF activates VEGFR2/PI3K/AKT pathway in osteoblasts
AKT is essential for 5-HMF-mediated osteoprotection
5-HMF inhibits glucocorticoid-induced apoptosis via Bax/Bcl-2 regulation
results: VEGFR2 phosphorylation enhanced and angiogenesis restored in vitro and in vivo
AKT inhibition eliminated protective effects
Bax/Bcl-2 expression modulation confirmed apoptosis inhibition

title: Impact of the CYFIP2 R87C variant in a human neuronal model in vitro
task: Characterize CYFIP2 R87C variant effects on neuronal development
model: (experiment) CRISPR/Cas9-edited hPSC-derived neurons and cortical organoids
inputs: R87C variant in CYFIP2; differentiation protocol into NPCs and cortical neurons
outputs: Neural morphology, motility, electrophysiology, protein expression, progenitor pool status
key_results: Mutant NPCs showed reduced motility and altered lamellipodia; R87C/R87C organoids displayed premature progenitor depletion (absent SOX2+ cells by day 30) and increased size, while 2D cultures showed minimal phenotype
comments: The stark divergence between 2D monolayer and 3D organoid phenotypes underscores the importance of physiological context; the mechanistic link to epileptic encephalopathy remains speculative.
hypotheses: R87C variant impairs NPC cytoskeletal dynamics
R87C variant affects cortical progenitor pool maintenance in 3D systems
Altered lamellipodia formation compromises cell motility
results: Reduced NPC motility and altered cell morphology in 2D
Premature SOX2+ progenitor depletion and organoid enlargement in 3D
Decreased CYFIP2 protein in R87C organoids

title: Intellectual disability-causing mutations in KIF11 impair microtubule dynamics and dendritic arborization
task: Elucidate KIF11 function in microtubule dynamics and dendritic development
model: (experiment) live-imaging in postmitotic neurons; photo-controllable KIF11 optogenetic tool
inputs: KIF11 depletion or MCLID mutations (Y82F, ΔCterm); neuronal cultures
outputs: MT dynamics (minus-end-out), dendritic arborization, mEPSC frequency, MT crosslinking, sliding velocity, ATP affinity
key_results: KIF11 depletion increased MT dynamics; MCLID mutants reduced MT dynamics and dendritic growth; ΔCterm disrupted tetramer formation and MT crosslinking; Y82F reduced sliding velocity and ATP affinity
comments: The identification of KIF11 as a 'MT dynamics rheostat' is elegant, though the direct causal relationship between MT dynamics changes and dendritic arborization deficits could be further mechanistically dissected.
hypotheses: KIF11 suppresses minus-end-out MT dynamics in mature neurons
MCLID mutations reduce KIF11-mediated MT regulation
MT dynamics control dendritic arborization and synaptic function
results: KIF11 depletion increased MT dynamics; MCLID mutants decreased dynamics
ΔCterm impaired tetramer/crosslinking; Y82F reduced sliding/ATP affinity
Reduced MT dynamics correlated with impaired dendritic growth and mEPSC frequency

title: Loss of polarity by Cdc42 depletion and oncogenic Kras activation in the mouse intestinal epithelia leads to a necrotizing enterocolitis (NEC)-like disease
task: Determine NEC pathogenesis via polarity loss and Kras activation in intestinal epithelium
model: (experiment) Cdc42-knockout + oncogenic Kras mouse intestinal epithelial model
inputs: Cdc42 loss; oncogenic Kras activation; initiation from stem cells or differentiated epithelium
outputs: Intestinal architecture, stem cell population, YAP/Hippo signaling, inflammatory cytokines, necroptotic cell death, transcriptomic signatures
key_results: Combined Cdc42 loss + Kras activation caused intestinal failure, stem cell loss, elevated IL-1/TNFα, necroptotic activation; suppression of YAP, IL-1, TNFα, or necroptosis rescued pathology
comments: Single-cell transcriptomics reveals coordinated dysregulation, and therapeutic rescue experiments provide strong mechanistic support, though the relative contribution of each pathway to lethality remains quantitatively unspecified.
hypotheses: Cdc42 loss + Kras activation disrupt polarity-dependent epithelial homeostasis
YAP hyperactivation drives pathology downstream of polarity loss
IL-1/TNFα-mediated inflammation and necroptosis are key executors of epithelial damage
results: Combined Cdc42/Kras mutation caused polarity loss, stem cell depletion, and NEC-like phenotype
YAP, IL-1, TNFα inhibition and necroptosis blockade all rescued pathology
Single-cell transcriptomics confirmed dysregulation of polarity, inflammatory, and necroptotic programs

title: From blink to care: smartphone video–based functional analysis and personalized management in pediatric blepharoptosis
task: Smartphone-based detection and management of pediatric blepharoptosis
model: N/A
inputs: Smartphone blink video clips and facial images (3164 clips, 1229 images)
outputs: Morphological measurements, levator dysfunction classification, ptosis management recommendations
key_results: Morphological ICC >0.90 vs manual; functional AUC 0.993; stratification accuracy 0.91 internal/0.89 real-world; patient satisfaction 4.93/5
comments: Clinical translation is strong but generalizability across diverse smartphone hardware and lighting conditions remains underexplored.
hypotheses: Smartphone video can accurately measure ptosis morphology comparable to manual clinical assessment
Machine learning can reliably identify levator dysfunction from blink dynamics
AI dialogue models improve patient education and decision-making in oculoplastic care
results: ICC >0.90 confirms morphological accuracy
AUC 0.993 demonstrates high functional classification performance
Patient satisfaction 4.93/5 indicates practical utility

title: Stabilizing updates in differentially private stochastic gradient descent with buffered rejection
task: Improve convergence and accuracy in differentially private stochastic gradient descent
model: FLDP training (fractional-order learning with differential privacy)
inputs: Sensitive training data with strict privacy budget constraints
outputs: Model parameters, test accuracy, privacy guarantee (membership inference ROC)
key_results: Test accuracy improvement 0.5–2 percentage points over baseline DPSGD; membership inference AUC ~0.5; faster convergence at same privacy level
comments: Buffered rejection is intuitive but the phased decay strategy introduces additional hyperparameters that may complicate tuning in practice.
hypotheses: Buffered comparison of candidate updates reduces noise-induced instability in selective updates
Phased parameter decay tracks training dynamics and improves convergence under tight privacy budgets
Privacy budget is not weakened by accuracy gains (privacy-utility tradeoff)
results: Consistent 0.5–2 pp accuracy improvement on MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, CIFAR-10, IMDb
Faster convergence at identical privacy budget
Membership inference AUC near 0.5 confirms privacy preservation

title: Canine gait analysis using inertial sensors and deep learning for orthopedic and neurological disorders
task: Distinguish neurological from orthopedic gait disorders in dogs using inertial sensors
model: Deep learning on inertial measurement unit (IMU) data
inputs: Wearable inertial sensor readings during gait (29 dogs)
outputs: Multi-class classification (healthy/orthopedic/neurological) and binary (healthy/non-healthy)
key_results: Multiclass accuracy 0.96; binary accuracy 0.85 on unseen dogs; robust generalization demonstrated
comments: Small sample size (29 dogs) and lack of cross-breed or cross-gait-speed validation limits immediate clinical deployment.
hypotheses: Inertial sensor patterns distinguish neurological from orthopedic gait impairments
Deep learning models trained on canine gait can generalize to unseen individuals
results: 0.96 multiclass accuracy achieved
0.85 generalization accuracy on unseen dogs

title: Gene expression links consistent immune and inflammatory pathways to bovine respiratory disease in high-risk stocker cattle
task: Identify immune and inflammatory pathways associated with bovine respiratory disease using transcriptomics
model: RNA-Seq differential expression analysis with functional enrichment
inputs: Whole-blood RNA from 60 high-risk stocker heifers across multiple timepoints; metaphylaxis treatment (tulathromycin or control)
outputs: Differentially expressed genes (DEGs), pathway enrichment, candidate biomarkers for BRD
key_results: >2000 DEGs at disease onset (FDR ≤0.05); five consistent biomarkers (IL1R2, HP, S100A9, TLR4, ALOX15); minimal transcriptomic differences between metaphylaxis groups
comments: Low morbidity rate in cohort prevented prediction of disease development; heat shock proteins emerged as potential severity markers but require validation.
hypotheses: BRD-affected cattle show distinct immune and inflammatory transcriptomic signatures at clinical onset
Metaphylaxis reduces BRD incidence but does not fundamentally alter disease-associated gene expression patterns
Consistent biomarkers exist across treatment groups for BRD detection
results: >2000 DEGs enriched in cytokine signaling, lymphocyte activation, inflammatory response
Five genes consistently dysregulated regardless of treatment
Minimal predictive power at study entry due to low morbidity

title: Modified Smith predictor-based fuzzy self-tuning FOPID controller for mean arterial pressure regulation
task: Regulate mean arterial pressure and cardiac output with adaptive control under time-varying delays
model: Modified Smith predictor (MSP) with fuzzy self-tuning fractional-order PID (FLOPID) controller
inputs: Patient hemodynamic state; drug infusions (sodium nitroprusside, dopamine); bounded variable delays (≤60 s)
outputs: Optimal drug infusion rates for MAP and CO regulation; closed-loop control signals
key_results: Faster rise/settling times, reduced overshoot, lower integral error vs conventional PID and FOPID; clinically acceptable performance under parameter uncertainty and disturbances
comments: Simulation-based validation only; clinical trial data and comparison to predictive/MPC strategies remain absent.
hypotheses: Fractional-order PID with fuzzy self-tuning improves transient and steady-state control of hemodynamics
Modified Smith predictor compensates for time-varying drug–response delays and maintains stability
Integrated FLST–FOPID–MSP architecture achieves clinically safe automated drug infusion
results: Faster rise and settling times vs baseline
Reduced overshoot and integral error metrics
Robust performance under inter-patient variability and measurement noise

title: Stabilizing updates in differentially private stochastic gradient descent with buffered rejection
task: differentially private training with improved convergence
model: buffered rejection mechanism + phased parameter decay
inputs: sensitive data, privacy budget
outputs: trained model weights, test accuracy
key_results: 0.5–2 percentage point accuracy improvement over standard DP-SGD at identical privacy budgets; faster convergence; AUC ≈ 0.5 on membership inference attacks
comments: The buffering strategy is clever for reducing variance in rejection decisions, though the phased decay introduces additional hyperparameters that may require tuning.
hypotheses: Buffered comparison reduces instability of selective updates
Phased decay matches training stages and improves accuracy
Empirical privacy is maintained despite accuracy gains
results: Confirmed by consistent improvements on MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, CIFAR-10, IMDb
Confirmed by tracking validation accuracy with adjusted noise/learning rate

title: CNN-LSTM model optimized by improved sparrow search algorithm for oil well production prediction
task: oil well production forecasting with hyperparameter optimization
model: ISSA-optimized CNN-LSTM
inputs: production data, operational parameters
outputs: daily oil production predictions
key_results: All performance metrics outperform ISSA-LSTM and GWO-LSTM baselines; accuracy satisfies practical oilfield requirements

CNN-LSTM combines feature extraction and temporal modeling effectively

title: Analytical investigation of soliton dynamics in the Fokas–Lenells equation with generalized self-phase modulation and nonlinear dispersion
task: analytical soliton solutions for nonlinear optical equations
model: Fokas–Lenells equation with generalized self-phase modulation and nonlinear dispersion
inputs: nonlinear optical media parameters
outputs: soliton solutions (dark, periodic, singular varieties)
key_results: Multiple solution families obtained via three analytical methods; extended simple equation approach is most effective; physical behavior visualized in 2D/3D plots
comments: The paper is primarily analytical; no numerical validation or experimental comparison of solution stability under perturbations is provided.
hypotheses: Modified extended tanh method yields diverse compact solutions
Extended simple equation method provides efficient solution construction
Generalized SPM and nonlinear dispersion enable soliton compression and frequency shifting

title: A new good point set stepwise shrinkage optimization in machine learning model for fog node performance prediction
task: hyperparameter optimization for fog computing performance prediction
model: Good Point Set Stepwise Shrinkage optimization
inputs: fog computing node performance data from IoT devices
outputs: optimized hyperparameters, node performance predictions
key_results: SVM MSE 4.061, BP MSE 4.114, CNN MSE 3.963 ± 0.0323; simpler than Sequential Uniform Designs; reduces cross-validation uncertainty
comments: The proposed method is essentially a structured design-of-experiments approach; the modest MSE values lack context without baseline comparisons or normalization by data scale.
hypotheses: Good Point Set Stepwise Shrinkage reduces cross-validation uncertainty
Structured search space improves model generalization
Lower computational cost achieved without sacrificing accuracy

Supported by MSE results
Supported by comparison to Sequential Uniform Designs

title: Prediction model of lost circulation based on drilling parameters with PSO-BP neural network
task: early lost circulation prediction in drilling

PSO initialization improves BP convergence and stability
Hybrid PSO-BP is superior to other metaheuristic-BP variants for this application

title: Spatiotemporal prediction of chlorophyll-a in semi-enclosed gulfs using a hybrid graph neural network-transformer framework with satellite data and causal analysis
task: spatiotemporal chlorophyll-a prediction with causal discovery
model: Graph Neural Network Transformer (GNN-T) with convergent cross-mapping
inputs: MODIS/Aqua and ERA5 satellite data, 300,000 observations
outputs: spatiotemporal Chl-a concentration predictions, causal relationships, bloom risk levels
key_results: Test R² = 0.906 (Gulf of Mexico); outperforms CNN-LSTM, BiLSTM, Temporal-Relational GNN, AGTCNSD; causality-driven feature selection achieves R² = 0.882 with 25% reduced computational cost; identifies SST, fluorescence height, POC as key drivers
comments: The integration of causal inference (CCM, Sobol) with model ablation is methodologically sound; generalization across gulfs strengthens claims, and uncertainty quantification via Monte Carlo dropout is rigorous.
hypotheses: GNN-T captures spatiotemporal dependencies better than standard RNNs
Causal discovery methods identify true mechanistic drivers vs. correlations
Causality-driven ablation preserves predictive accuracy with fewer variables
Probabilistic risk thresholds support operational decision-making
results: Confirmed by R² = 0.906 and superior metrics vs. baselines
Confirmed by CCM elucidating nonlinear causal relationships
Confirmed by R² = 0.882 with 25% cost reduction
Inferred from quartile-based bloom thresholds and confidence intervals

title: YOLO-Starfish: fish object detection learning complex underwater features
task: underwater fish object detection with complex environmental conditions
model: YOLO-Starfish (YOLOv8 + C2Star module + Attention-driven Enhancement Module)
inputs: underwater images with poor quality, randomness, occlusion
outputs: fish species and bounding box detections
key_results: Strong performance on RUOD and new Underwater Freshwater Fish Dataset (UFFD: 16,904 images, 19 species); excellent results on COCO2017; C2Star and ADEM improve robustness
comments: The C2Star module's element-wise multiplication approach to mimic optical degradation is intuitive; however, no quantitative metrics (mAP, precision/recall) are provided in the abstract.
hypotheses: C2Star captures physical degradation characteristics of underwater optics
ADEM mitigates channel imbalance and improves feature robustness
YOLOv8 backbone with these modules generalizes across underwater datasets and COCO
results: Inferred from model design and underwater optical physics
Inferred from adaptive enhancement strategy
Confirmed by strong performance on multiple benchmarks (RUOD, UFFD, COCO2017)

title: Large-scale multi-omics profiling reveals environmental and evolutionary drivers of fungal phylogeographic and metabolic diversity
task: identify environmental and evolutionary drivers of fungal metabolic diversity
model: comparative genomics and metabolomics across phylogeographic populations
inputs: 1052 Aspergillus flavus strains from four continents with genomic and metabolomic data
outputs: phylogeographic metabolic patterns, biosynthetic gene clusters, specialized metabolite profiles
key_results: low-mycotoxin clades produce distinct uncharacterized metabolites; regulatory and primary metabolic changes, not just biosynthetic gene variation, explain 70%+ of metabolite profile differentiation
comments: Multi-omics integration is thorough, but causal mechanisms underlying regulatory changes remain incompletely specified.
hypotheses: environmental ecological niches drive fungal metabolic differentiation through selection
biosynthetic gene cluster variation is the primary driver of specialized metabolite diversity
regulatory and primary metabolic changes account for metabolic differentiation independent of biosynthetic pathway genes
results: strong phylogeographic metabolic patterns associated with specific ecological niches confirmed
biosynthetic gene variation only partially explains metabolic differences; regulatory variation is primary driver
low-mycotoxin clades produce alternative specialized metabolites via distinct biosynthetic pathways
